Black members of Oklahoma Native American tribe file federal lawsuit to gain citizenship rights
items.summary: Two descendants of Black people once enslaved by a Native American tribe are asking a federal court in Oklahoma to force tribal leaders to issue them citizenship cards as ordered a year ago by a tribal court

items.title: Protesters burst in and take over news conference as Madison police identify man killed by officer a day earlier
items.summary: Protesters burst in and take over news conference as Madison police identify man killed by officer a day earlier

items.title: Giant metal roosters spread autism acceptance in Maine after town zoning spat
items.summary: Giant metal roosters are popping up on lawns in and around a coastal Maine community and as far away as New Zealand in support of a 6-year-old autistic girl and her love of the colorful creations

items.title: Liberal Justice Elena Kagan says the Supreme Court is not a rubber stamp for Trump
items.summary: Justice Elena Kagan says the Supreme Court may be deeply conservative but it is not simply a rubber stamp for President Donald Trump

items.title: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul explains why she signed the first statewide moratorium on data centers
items.summary: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul said she felt the need to sign the nation’s first statewide moratorium on data center development because tech giants “are flooding the zone” with construction efforts faster than states and localities can keep up.

items.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
items.summary: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

items.title: Two men stabbed near Central Park in New York City
items.summary: Two men were stabbed near Central Park, about a block apart. Authorities say the victims, both men in their 50s, are stable following the incident.

items.title: President Trump announces new tariffs on dozens of countries
items.summary: President Trump announced new 10% to 12.5% tariffs on 60 countries over accusations of "forced labor." Those new duties will take effect at 12:01 a.m. ET on Friday morning.

items.title: Man who stabbed 2 near New York City’s Central Park yelled ‘Allahu Akbar,’ police say
items.summary: The stabbings occurred about a block apart in Manhattan’s Upper West Side, law enforcement sources said.

items.title: U.S. sets new tariffs on dozens of countries as temporary duties are set to expire
items.summary: The U.S. Trade Rep announced a wave of tariffs on 60 trade partners in order to replace President Donald Trump’s temporary tariff which expires at midnight.

items.title: Victim is identified in deadly Madison police shooting as tensions rise
items.summary: MADISON, Wis. — With protests and demands for accountability growing louder in Madison, Wisconsin, officials have identified the man who was fatally shot by a police officer this week.

items.title: Justice Department agrees to withdraw subpoenas of New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
items.summary: A judge repeatedly questioned why prosecutors had issued the subpoenas before pursuing alternative investigative steps.

items.title: The 33 best dorm essentials to make the school year go smoothly — at least as your room is concerned
items.summary: We found the best dorm essentials to get this year, including bedding, organizers, decor, tech, supplies and much more.

items.title: Oil prices surge as Congress splits on Iran war votes: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: In today’s edition, we examine how the escalating Iran war is playing out in the oil markets and the halls of the Capitol. Plus, Scott Wong and Matt Dixon have a profile of the little-known new senator from South Carolina.

items.title: How to keep an ancient text interesting to students? Make it a summer blockbuster
items.summary: English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.

items.title: Egyptian warrior princesses, ancient avocados, and more science news
items.summary: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.

items.title: Trump cuts target federal advisory boards. What does it mean for agencies like NASA?
items.summary: The Trump administration has terminated or suspended more than 100 federal advisory committees. Former members say agencies like NASA now have less accountability to stakeholders and the public.

items.title: Invasive species cause more harm in the Global South
items.summary: Invasive species can wreak havoc on ecosystems. A new study in Science suggests low- and middle-income countries in the Global South experience greater harms.

items.title: 2 dead in flash floods caused by heavy rain in West Virginia, governor's office says
items.summary: Two people have died in flash floods caused by torrential rains in West Virginia this week

items.title: Trump allies are poised to weaken protections for historic sites — not just in D.C.
items.summary: A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.

items.title: What is deodorant concealer and is the viral product safe? Derms weigh in
items.summary: Deodorant concealer can temporarily hide dark underarms, but dermatologists warn that it may irritate skin or mask deeper issues. Here’s what to know before you try it.

items.title: House votes to end Iran war, but Senate Republicans vote to let Trump continue waging it
items.summary: The House approved a nonbinding proposal to end the Iran war, directing President Donald Trump to remove armed forces from hostilities.

items.title: More than 24 states sue Trump administration after it put conditions on receiving disaster funding
items.summary: More than two dozen states have sued the Trump administration after it required them to comply with election and immigration conditions to get billions of dollars in federal funding for disasters

items.title: Nun who was detained by ICE while walking to Mass in Texas says many are in her situation
items.summary: A nun who was taken into immigration custody after being arrested while walking to Mass in her habit last month in South Texas says she wants to speak out to help others

items.title: For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump
items.summary: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.

items.title: Chadwick Boseman’s brothers seek to remove his widow from control of late actor’s estate
items.summary: Chadwick Boseman’s brothers filed a petition last week seeking control of the “Black Panther” star’s estate, accusing their brother’s widow of mismanagement and failing to distribute the assets.

items.title: PT Organics fruit pouches recalled over possible soft strands of plastic in purée
items.summary: More than 380,000 fruit pouches are being recalled nationwide over concerns they may contain soft strands of plastic in the purée.

items.title: Captain pleads guilty in boat crash that killed 3 girls from Miami sailing camp
items.summary: A tugboat captain has pleaded guilty to causing a deadly Miami collision that killed three girls from a sailing camp last summer

items.title: Member of Vance’s Secret Service detail removed amid probe into alleged compromise of security info
items.summary: A member of JD Vance’s protective detail has been removed pending an investigation into allegations of compromising the vice president’s security, a Secret Service spokesman said Thursday.

items.title: White House says Saudi Arabia nuclear deal contingent on joining Abraham Accords
items.summary: During a press briefing, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt echoed a social media post from President Trump that said any nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia would be contingent on them joining the Abraham Accords. NBC News' Monica Alba reports from the White House on the potential deal and the White House's approach.

items.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
items.summary: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.

items.title: This popular Dyson vacuum is $290 off at Walmart right now — get it before the price goes back up
items.summary: Walmart has the Dyson V8 Absolute Cordless Vacuum marked down to just under $300 — a rare discount on a Dyson vacuum. Here’s why shoppers love it.

items.title: Republicans urge Justice Department to investigate former prosecutor Jack Smith
items.summary: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.

items.title: Wisconsin governor calls for transparency after Madison officer fatally shoots man with knife
items.summary: The police shooting death of a man with a knife has roiled a Wisconsin city and prompted the governor to call for transparency and accountability in any investigation

items.title: Macy’s Black Friday in July sale — save up to 70% on bestselling brands
items.summary: During the Macy’s Black Friday in July sale you can save on skin care, apparel, fragrance, appliances and more.

items.title: FDA Advisory Committee to Review Peptide Restrictions Underway
items.summary: Peptides are often being promoted by wellness influencers and podcasters as treatments for everything from injuries and aging and now an FDA panel is meeting to discuss whether to loosen restrictions and make them easier to get. But critics say there's not enough data on safety and efficacy. NBC’s medical contributor Natalie Azar joins TODAY to break down the seven peptides being discussed and how effective they are.

items.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
items.summary: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact requires kingdom to normalize Israel relations through Abraham Accords
items.summary: President Donald Trump says the Saudi nuclear agreement requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords

items.title: Protests Erupt After Fatal Police Shooting of Man in Wisconsin
items.summary: An investigation is underway after a police officer in Madison, Wisconsin, fatally shot a man who investigators say pulled a knife during a scuffle with deputies. Police say it took place after the man fled from officers who were responding to a report of an individual allegedly stealing bicycles and checking car doors. NBC’s Shaquille Brewster reports for TODAY. Warning: Video of the incident is disturbing.

items.title: Man who pleaded guilty to killing top Minnesota Democratic lawmaker and her husband to be sentenced
items.summary: The Minnesota man who pleaded guilty to killing a top Democratic state lawmaker and her husband is due to be sentenced in federal court

items.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Pushes West After Battering Louisiana
items.summary: Tropical Storm Bertha is pushing toward Texas after crashing ashore in Louisiana. The Gulf Coast was hammered with heavy rain, high winds and floods. Meanwhile, some areas are set to face triple digit temperatures through the weekend. NBC’s George Solis reports and TODAY’s Al Roker tracks the latest forecast.

items.title: US on track to have historically low number of homicides in 2026, report says
items.summary: A new report from the Council on Criminal Justice shows an 18% decrease in the homicide rate across 30 American cities from 2025 compared to the same period in 2026

items.title: Tropical Storm Bertha makes landfall in Louisiana and moves toward Texas
items.summary: Tropical Storm Bertha has made landfall in Louisiana

items.title: Navy says it will no longer release sailors' service history
items.summary: The U.S. Navy is restricting press access to information about service members by no longer releasing details about sailors' service history

items.title: What to know about the autopsy and investigation into Nolan Wells' death
items.summary: The family of Nolan Xavier Wells is still grappling for answers about the 18-year-old athlete's death

items.title: ICE drops plan to convert 2 Pennsylvania warehouses into detention centers
items.summary: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement is giving up on its plan to use two rural Pennsylvania warehouses to hold thousands of people

items.title: Passengers in ICE shooting say officers not in danger, driver was parked
items.summary: Two witnesses have told a federal court the immigration agent who fatally shot a Mexican man in Houston on July 7 was in no danger of being run over

items.title: Wisconsin police officer shoots, kills man on city street near state Capitol
items.summary: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest

items.title: R. James Woolsey, CIA director during the Clinton administration, dies at 84

items.title: Judge questions difference between Hegseth's testosterone policy and transgender care for troops
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s new initiative to test and treat troops for low testosterone is facing scrutiny from a federal judge

items.title: Florida researchers bring endangered elkhorn coral ashore to protect them
items.summary: University of Miami scientists are working to save an endangered coral species from extinction by bringing them ashore

items.title: US-Canada bridge has been years in the making. But tension between countries is sapping the joy
items.summary: The rocky relationship between the U.S. and Canada is spoiling the celebration of an extraordinary project between the two countries

items.title: Demand for critical minerals fuels conflict but could help resource-rich nations, WTO says
items.summary: The head of the World Trade Organization says surging global demand for critical minerals should be “a once-in-a-lifetime chance” for resource-rich countries in Africa and elsewhere to harness wealth and improve life for millions

items.title: Endangered right whales could wait almost a decade for new US protections
items.summary: New protections for endangered North Atlantic right whales are almost certain to face a nearly decade-long delay

items.title: Rubio says any nuclear agreement the US signs will not lead to nuclear proliferation
items.summary: The Trump administration is expected to sign an agreement with Saudi Arabia that could potentially provide the kingdom with uranium enrichment capability for its civilian nuclear program

items.title: Nolan Wells’ independent autopsy results in ‘undetermined’ cause and manner of death
items.summary: The doctor who performed the autopsy said in the report that he couldn’t rule out foul play in the Mississippi teen’s death.

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
items.summary: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

items.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
items.summary: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
